D9 BiologyL5 EvaluateReference

Morpho Butterfly — Christmas-Tree Multilayer

no blue pigment; Morpho wing scales carry a Christmas-tree-shaped chitin/air multilayer that constructively interferes at ~470 nm — pure structural blue, brightest in nature

Vukusic 2003 detailed the Morpho rhetenor wing scale: a series of chitin ridges with alternating chitin (n=1.56) and air (n=1.0) layers stacked like Christmas-tree branches. The Bragg condition 2(n₁d₁ + n₂d₂) = mλ at the chosen layer thicknesses reflects ~470 nm blue at normal incidence — and the multiple-angle geometry keeps the colour broad across viewing angles.

Drag chitin layer thickness; the reflected peak wavelength shifts.
